text: Deep learning models such as Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) have gained incredible amount of attention in recent times due to their extraordinary results in time series forecasting. Deep learning models take only few hours to implement while results are available within few minutes to hours. The performance of deep learning models relies heavily on quality of training data. It is not always possible to get large amount of high quality training data. Time series forecasting models such as ARIMA, SARIMA and Prophet require minimum few months training data. Therefore it becomes necessary to increase the amount of available data in order to train models effectively so as to produce optimum results. Data augmentation is a technique that increases amount of available data in training dataset by applying small perturbations to input data. These perturbations can be noise addition, sequence permutations, learnable sequence transformations or generation of new sequences. This survey paper reviews time series generation and models. The characteristics and taxonomies of various time series generation models are discussed. This paper not only compares the efficiencies of different architectural variants, but also analyzes the various evaluation metrics and discusses their challenges and drawbacks. Finally, it summarizes the various models and their contributions to time series generation, acting as a reference guide for further works.
explanation: Cohesive, survey-like prose with some awkward phrasing (e.g., 'minimum few months'), a common signature of AI-generated drafts. However, it could also be human-written academic text.
